questions.question: Which CLI protocol is cleartext and disabled by default on a WLC?
questions.explanation: Telnet is legacy cleartext CLI access; disabled by default on WLC. SSH (encrypted) is enabled by default.

questions.question: What does AAA stand for?
questions.explanation: AAA stands for Authentication, Authorization, Accounting.

questions.question: Which AAA protocol is Cisco-proprietary?
questions.explanation: TACACS+ (Terminal Access Controller Access-Control System Plus) is the Cisco AAA protocol for admin authentication/authorization/accounting.

questions.question: Which AAA protocol is industry-standard and used for 802.1X client auth?
questions.explanation: RADIUS (Remote Authentication Dial-In User Service) is the industry-standard AAA protocol used for 802.1X client auth and admin login.

questions.question: What is the only way to reach a WLC before an IP address is configured?
questions.explanation: Console is direct RJ-45 or USB serial access; only way to reach WLC before an IP address is configured.

questions.question: What is out-of-band management?
options: Management traffic carried on a separate physical interface from data traffic
Management traffic that uses TLS encryption
Management restricted to the default VLAN
questions.explanation: Out-of-band management is management traffic carried on a separate physical interface from data traffic (e.g., WLC service port).

questions.question: Which SNMP version adds authentication and encryption?
questions.explanation: SNMPv3 adds authentication/encryption; configurable in WLC Management tab. v1 and v2 do not.

questions.question: What is a CPU ACL on a WLC?
options: An ACL applied to the WLC CPU to restrict Telnet/SSH/HTTP/HTTPS/SNMP sessions to the WLC
An ACL that filters only CAPWAP traffic
A per-user policy on wireless clients
A QoS policy for inter-AP traffic
questions.explanation: CPU ACL is an ACL applied to the WLC CPU to restrict which devices can open Telnet/SSH/HTTP/HTTPS/SNMP sessions to the WLC.

questions.question: What does 'Management via wireless' control on a WLC?
options: Whether a wireless client can reach the WLC's management interface
Whether SSH is enabled over the air
Whether APs can push firmware wirelessly
questions.explanation: Management via wireless is a WLC setting (disabled by default) that controls whether a wireless client can reach the WLC's management interface.

questions.question: What is cloud-managed AP/WLC access?
options: Management via a cloud dashboard (e.g., Cisco Meraki) rather than local CLI/GUI
Management via TACACS+ in a private cloud
Management restricted to the default gateway
questions.explanation: Cloud-managed is management via a cloud dashboard (e.g., Cisco Meraki) rather than local CLI/GUI.

questions.question: Why might a browser show a 'certificate authority invalid' warning when connecting to a WLC via HTTPS?
options: The WLC presents a self-signed certificate; benign on a local trusted network
The TLS version is out of date
The WLC has no IP configured
questions.explanation: Certificate authority invalid warning is a browser warning seen when the WLC presents a self-signed certificate; benign on a local trusted network.

questions.question: What is Syslog used for?
options: Logging protocol for forwarding messages to a log server
Protocol for tagging VLANs on trunks
questions.explanation: Syslog is a logging protocol for forwarding messages to a log server.
